---
title: "Lecture 21: DOM Manipulation using JavaScript"
subtitle: "{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}"
author: "{{< meta instructor.name >}}"
date: "February 25, 2026"
code-annotations: below
---

## {#title-slide data-menu-title="Title Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="custom-title">
[Lecture 21]{.baby-blue-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle">
[DOM Manipulation using JavaScript]{.white-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Week 8 | Unit II: JavaScript & DOM<br>
{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}<br>
{{< meta instructor.name >}}, {{< meta instructor.title >}}
</div>

---

## {data-menu-title="Learning Objectives"}

[Learning Objectives]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Explain what DOM is and why it matters
- Select and manipulate DOM elements safely
- Handle user events and update UI dynamically
- Build small reusable DOM utilities
- Apply best practices for performance and maintainability
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="What is DOM"}

[What is the DOM?]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
**DOM (Document Object Model)** is the browser representation of HTML as a tree of objects.

With JavaScript, we can:

- Read content and attributes
- Change styles and classes
- Create, insert, and remove elements
- React to user actions in real time
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="DOM Tree"}

[DOM Tree Concept]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
  A[document] --> B[html]
  B --> C[head]
  B --> D[body]
  D --> E[header]
  D --> F[main]
  F --> G[section]
  G --> H[p]
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Selecting Elements"}

[Selecting Elements]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-2|3-4|5-6"}
const title = document.getElementById('page-title');
const card = document.querySelector('.card');

const buttons = document.querySelectorAll('button.action');
const items = document.getElementsByClassName('item');

const links = document.getElementsByTagName('a');
```

::: {.fragment .body-text-m}
Prefer `querySelector` / `querySelectorAll` for flexible CSS-style selection.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Updating Content"}

[Updating Content and Attributes]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-2|4-5|7-8"}
const msg = document.querySelector('#message');
msg.textContent = 'Welcome to Web Technology';

const hero = document.querySelector('img.hero');
hero.setAttribute('alt', 'Campus building image');

const profile = document.querySelector('#profile-link');
profile.href = '/resource/index.qmd';
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Classes and Styles"}

[Classes and Styles]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-2|3-4|6-7"}
const panel = document.querySelector('.status-panel');
panel.classList.add('active');
panel.classList.remove('hidden');
panel.classList.toggle('expanded');

panel.style.borderColor = '#0b63ce';
panel.style.backgroundColor = '#f2f7ff';
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Create and Remove"}

[Creating and Removing Elements]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-3|5-6|8"}
const list = document.querySelector('#todo-list');
const li = document.createElement('li');
li.textContent = 'Review DOM selectors';

list.appendChild(li);
list.prepend(document.createElement('hr'));

li.remove();
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Events"}

[Event Handling Basics]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-2|4-7"}
const saveBtn = document.querySelector('#save-btn');
saveBtn.addEventListener('click', handleSave);

function handleSave(event) {
  event.preventDefault();
  document.querySelector('#status').textContent = 'Saved successfully';
}
```

::: {.fragment .body-text-m}
Common events: `click`, `input`, `change`, `submit`, `keydown`.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Mini Example"}

[Mini Example: Live Character Counter]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-2|4-9"}
const textarea = document.querySelector('#bio');
const counter = document.querySelector('#count');

textarea.addEventListener('input', () => {
  const n = textarea.value.length;
  counter.textContent = `${n} characters`;
  counter.classList.toggle('warn', n > 120);
});
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Best Practices"}

[Best Practices]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Cache reused element references
- Use event delegation for dynamic lists
- Prefer class toggling over frequent inline style updates
- Keep DOM updates minimal inside loops
- Separate logic from presentation where possible
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Summary"}

[Summary]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
In this lecture, we learned to:

- Navigate the DOM tree
- Select and update elements
- Create and remove nodes
- Handle events to make pages interactive

These skills are the bridge from static pages to dynamic web applications.
:::
